mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing present valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden unemployment rate increase above 4.0 percent signals immediate labor market deterioration and recession probability, triggering rapid risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 4.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand contraction, reducing corporate earnings expectations and driving negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2 percent year-over-year during inflationary periods signals supply-side bottlenecks that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 2 percent yields positive 20-day returns in >60% of observations during high inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with stable unemployment rates below 4.0 percent indicate a successful soft landing strategy, supporting equity valuations through sustained earnings growth without recession risk.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases while UNRATE remains below 4.0
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising rates and low unemployment produces negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent signals economic stagnation that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns as investors price in recessionary earnings cuts.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent CPI inflation above 3.0 percent erodes real corporate profit margins and forces prolonged monetary tightening, generating negative quarterly equity returns through higher discount rates and lower demand.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 3.0 percent correlates with positive 60-day returns in >55% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 200,000 during periods of high interest rates signals resilient labor demand that supports consumer spending and drives positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth above 200,000 during high rate periods yields negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases.
